model.label: Claude Sonnet 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Fast flagship Claude model for coding, analysis, and high-throughput agent use.

pick.rationale: Duke and Michigan enter the National Championship as the two most evenly matched teams in the bracket, separated by less than one point in adjusted efficiency margin (KenPom #1 at +39.17 vs. #2 at +38.38), with Duke holding fractional edges on both offense (AdjOE 128.0 vs. 126.7) and defense (AdjDE 88.7 vs. 88.4 — the nation's best unit). The Blue Devils won the regular-season head-to-head matchup 68-63 over Michigan on February 21 and have demonstrated throughout the tournament that they can win at an elite level without starting point guard Caleb Foster (fractured foot, season-ending), defeating Houston in the Final Four and navigating six tournament games with their deepest contributors stepping up. A headline flagging a potential new game-changing Duke injury that briefly shifted Michigan to the betting favorite introduces genuine uncertainty and caps confidence near a coin-flip, but Duke's marginal efficiency edge on both ends of the floor, the nation's best defense, and their proven head-to-head superiority over this specific Michigan team make the Blue Devils the narrow right side in what is effectively the most evenly contested championship game the bracket could produce.

reasoningStep.title: National Championship: Duke vs. Michigan — Full Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Duke (#1 KenPom/Torvik, AdjEM +39.17) vs. Michigan (#2 KenPom/Torvik, AdjEM +38.38) is the most evenly matched championship game in the bracket, with Duke holding fractional edges on both offense and defense. Duke won the regular-season head-to-head 68-63 on Feb. 21. Duke has navigated the entire tournament without Caleb Foster (fractured foot, season-ending), while a web headline suggested a new game-changing Duke injury shifted Michigan to betting favorite — but the specific injury could not be confirmed beyond previously known issues. Michigan's Yaxel Lendeborg has been playing through an ankle sprain. Duke's marginal efficiency edge and head-to-head win provide the narrowest of margins for the pick.
evidence: Duke: KenPom #1, AdjEM +39.17, AdjOE 127.9 (4th nationally), AdjDE 88.7 (1st nationally). Torvik #1, AdjOE 128.0 (4th), AdjDE 90.4 (1st).
Michigan: KenPom #2, AdjEM +38.38, AdjOE 126.7 (8th nationally), AdjDE 88.4 (2nd nationally). Torvik #2, AdjOE 127.7 (7th), AdjDE 90.5 (2nd).
Duke beat Michigan 68-63 on Feb. 21, 2026 in a regular-season thriller when Duke was ranked #3 and Michigan was #1.

MSN headline: 'Michigan is new NCAA title favorite after Duke suffers game-changing injury' — article returned 404, specific new injury unconfirmed beyond Foster (foot, out all tournament) and Brown (shoulder, playing through it).
Pre-tournament betting market had Michigan as the favorite at bet365 despite being the #2 seed; Duke +300, Michigan +360 at DraftKings — market viewed these teams as co-favorites throughout.
Duke defeated Houston in the Final Four (f4-1, confidence 0.67) and Michigan defeated Arizona (f4-2, confidence 0.54), both arriving via competitive semifinal wins.
